what_it_does: Records metadata for a channel attachment that points to an image asset in the design-assets channel.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
added timestamp is ISO 8601 with microsecond precision and UTC offset
standouts: The target URI includes a generic name plus a numeric qualifier: 'image (58)'
The file is purely a pointer/record with no embedded image data or description
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Target uses a custom image:// URI scheme
Includes an added timestamp for provenance
concerns: Target title 'image (58)' is generic and gives no semantic clue about the image
No alt text, dimensions, or format information
notes: A minimal metadata stub that links a design-assets channel attachment to a specific image asset by URI.
questions: What does the '(58)' in the target URI represent?
Is image:// a resolvable scheme or just an identifier?
How is this attachment related to a specific channel message or thread?
Was this file generated automatically or authored manually?
purpose_vs_actual: The fields imply it is an attachment record for an image, and the content is exactly that metadata.
intention_signals: Track an image attachment belonging to the design-assets channel so it can be referenced and retrieved by a stable id and target URI.
novelty: The target URI embeds a display name with a numeric qualifier rather than a content-derived or descriptive identifier.
contribution: A precise attachment-to-asset pointer with timestamp and type, useful for reconstructing which image belongs to which channel.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML-frontmatter attachment record for the design-assets channel, linking attachment id att-71ebc374 to image asset image://design-assets/icons/image (58) with a creation timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to catalog and reference a specific image asset within the design-assets channel.
